name: Network Policy Debugging
description: Debug and verify Kubernetes network policies to ensure traffic is correctly allowed or blocked between pods and services.
prerequisites: A CNI that supports NetworkPolicy (Calico, Cilium, etc.)

steps.title: List network policies in a namespace
steps.description: View all network policies and what pods they select.

steps.title: Inspect a network policy's rules
steps.description: View the ingress and egress rules of a specific policy.

steps.title: Test connectivity between pods
steps.description: Verify whether traffic is allowed or blocked between two pods.
steps.command: kubectl exec <source-pod> -- curl -s --connect-timeout 5 http://<target-service>:<port>/health || echo 'Connection blocked or failed'

steps.title: Check which policies apply to a pod
steps.description: Find all network policies that select a specific pod based on its labels.
steps.command: kubectl get networkpolicies -n <namespace> -o json | jq --arg pod_labels "$(kubectl get pod <pod-name> -n <namespace> -o jsonpath='{.metadata.labels}')" '.items[] | select(.spec.podSelector.matchLabels as $sel | all(to_entries[]; . as $e | ($pod_labels | fromjson)[$e.key] == $e.value)) | .metadata.name'

steps.title: Deploy a test pod for connectivity testing
steps.description: Launch a temporary pod with networking tools for manual testing.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Network Policy Debugging
set -euo pipefail

NS=${1:-"default"}

echo "=== Network Policies in $NS ==="
kubectl get networkpolicies -n "$NS" 2>/dev/null || echo "No network policies found"

echo ""
echo "=== Policy Details ==="
for policy in $(kubectl get networkpolicies -n "$NS" -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' 2>/dev/null); do
  echo "--- $policy ---"
  kubectl describe networkpolicy "$policy" -n "$NS" | grep -A20 'Spec:'
  echo ""
done

echo ""
echo "=== Debug Commands ==="
echo "Test connectivity: kubectl exec <pod> -n $NS -- curl -s --connect-timeout 5 http://<service>:<port>"
echo "Netshoot pod:      kubectl run nettest --rm -it --restart=Never --image=nicolaka/netshoot -n $NS -- bash"
echo "DNS test:          kubectl exec <pod> -n $NS -- nslookup <service>"

faq.question: What happens if no network policy selects a pod?
faq.answer: By default, if no NetworkPolicy selects a pod, all traffic is allowed. Once any NetworkPolicy selects a pod, all traffic not explicitly allowed by a policy is denied.

faq.question: How do I allow traffic from all namespaces?
faq.answer: Use a namespaceSelector with an empty matchLabels ({}) in the ingress/egress rule. This matches all namespaces.
